bibliographic.citation: Frank Jewett Mather Jr., 'King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone,' Publications of the Modern Language Association of America 12.1 (1897): i-lxvii, 1-150, doi:10.2307/456284.
bibliographic.container_title: Publications of the Modern Language Association of America
bibliographic.title: King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone

entities.description: The first publication of the Digby-based Middle English version. Mather's contribution includes preface and introduction on printed pp. i-lxvii, an unnumbered Digby f. 166r plate, and edited text with apparatus on pp. 1-150.
entities.label: Frank Jewett Mather Jr., King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone (1897)

rights.basis: The edition was first published in the United States in 1897; U.S. Copyright Office guidance confirms that works published before 1931 are in the public domain as of 2026.
rights.component: 1897 editorial text, introduction, apparatus, and typography

corpus-encoded edited text: printed pp. 1-150 = PDF pp. 85-234

entities.description: A complete 628-page scan of the bound PMLA volume. Mather's complete contribution occupies PDF pp. 13-234; its edited Middle English text occupies PDF pp. 85-234.

access.accessMethod: Embedded plate in the complete PMLA scan
access.entityLabel: Mather's facsimile plate of Digby 185, f. 166r
notes: The plate is at PDF pp. 82-83 of the cached complete volume and is not mislabeled as a complete manuscript facsimile.

entities.description: A single manuscript-page reproduction embedded between Mather's introduction and edited text, separately scoped from both the physical codex and the complete PMLA scan.
entities.label: Mather's facsimile plate of Digby 185, f. 166r

entities.description: The complete base witness for Mather's edition. The romance occupies ff. 166r-203r within the third of three apparently independent sections of a composite mid-fifteenth-century English codex.
entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Digby 185

physicalDescription.extent: ii + 203 + ii leaves; King Ponthus at ff. 166r-203r
notes: Composite of three apparently independent sections; the current description identifies one Anglicana formata scribe for the third section.
The explicit appears on f. 203r, followed by four blank lines; the rest of the leaf has been cut away.

rights.basis: The work and surviving witness text were created in the fifteenth century. This conclusion does not apply to modern photographs, editorial matter, or digital markup.

access.accessMethod: Exact shelfmark searches of Digital Bodleian and current catalog review
access.entityLabel: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Douce 384
notes: No complete public digital object was found. Request the entire sixty-two-leaf factitious composite, including binding, furniture, blanks, and all non-Ponthus fragments, rather than only ff. 1r-2v.

access.accessMethod: Bodleian Special Collections mediated copying or Imaging Services order
access.entityLabel: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Douce 384
access.format: Repository-supplied digital images subject to feasibility and quote
notes: Order scope should specify the complete physical MS Douce 384; identify ff. 1r-2v only as the work portion.

entities.description: A factitious composite manuscript of sixty-two leaves. Two nonconsecutive late-fifteenth-century paper leaves at ff. 1r-2v preserve fragments of this English Ponthus; Mather prints Fragment A and Fragment B within his apparatus.
entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Douce 384

physicalDescription.extent: Factitious composite of 62 leaves; Ponthus fragments at ff. 1r-2v
notes: ARLIMA assigns the Ponthus component to Flanders; that witness fact is not used to localize the English translation itself.
physicalDescription.support: Paper for the two Ponthus leaves

access.entityLabel: The Noble History of King Ponthus, Wynkyn de Worde (1511)

access.entityLabel: The Noble History of King Ponthus, Wynkyn de Worde (1511)
notes: First resolve the current shelfmark of Mather's imperfect Bodleian copy, then request the complete surviving bound object rather than only the pp. 57-60 source passage.

bibliographic.citation: The history of the moost excellent and myghty prince and hygh renowned knight kynge Ponthus of Galyce and lytell Brytaygne (London: Wynkyn de Worde, 1511).
bibliographic.title: The Noble History of King Ponthus

entities.description: The later English printed revision or retranslation labeled W by Mather. The Bodleian copy he used is imperfect, lacking A1-2; Mather reports an original extent of one hundred leaves, fifty-four woodcuts, and sixty chapters.

holding.notes: Mather consulted the Bodleian copy; its current shelfmark was not resolved in the public sources checked.

entities.label: The Noble History of King Ponthus, Wynkyn de Worde (1511)

entities.description: The late-fourteenth- or early-fifteenth-century French source work translated and adapted into Middle English. It is an abstract work entity, not any one surviving French manuscript or printed edition.
entities.label: Anonymous French prose romance Ponthus et Sidoine

access.accessMethod: Current Archives and Manuscripts record and digitised-content link review
access.entityLabel: London, British Library, Royal MS 15 E VI

access.accessMethod: British Library Digitisation Services order
access.entityLabel: London, British Library, Royal MS 15 E VI
access.format: Repository photography subject to suitability and quote

entities.description: Mather's principal French comparison manuscript R. Ponthus et Sidoine occupies ff. 207r-226v inside a 440-folio illustrated compilation made at Rouen in 1444-1445.
entities.label: London, British Library, Royal MS 15 E VI

physicalDescription.extent: 440 leaves plus flyleaves; Ponthus et Sidoine at ff. 207r-226v

access.accessMethod: Oxford University Research Archive direct PDF
access.entityLabel: Bodleian Quarto Catalogue description of MS Digby 185

localCopies.label: Complete Bodleian Digby quarto catalog PDF

bibliographic.citation: Bodleian Library, Quarto Catalogues, Digby manuscripts, MS 185 entry, printed pp. 195-196, with 1999 addenda p. 85.

entities.description: The historical Macray entry and 1999 addenda, including the codex extent, Ponthus locus and incipit/explicit, dating, and a disputed Hoccleve attribution.
entities.label: Bodleian Quarto Catalogue description of MS Digby 185

entities.description: Current specialist work and witness record distinguishing the anonymous English prose romance, Digby and Douce witnesses, French source, Mather edition, and Watson's later work.
entities.label: ARLIMA record for King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone

next_steps: Review Hunt and Watson's full catalog rationale and modern Hoccleve scholarship.
Keep the romance anonymous and the attribution disputed unless evidence addresses this component independently of neighboring Hoccleve texts.
openQuestions.question: What evidence underlies the 1999 catalog addendum's attribution of the English romance to Thomas Hoccleve?

next_steps: Submit a Bodleian mediated-copying or Imaging Services enquiry for the complete factitious composite, including binding, furniture, endleaves, blanks, and every fragment.
Map ff. 1r-2v as the Ponthus portion without reducing the request to those leaves.
openQuestions.question: Can a complete color surrogate of the entire sixty-two-leaf MS Douce 384 be obtained?

next_steps: Consult modern stemmatic work on Ponthus et Sidoine and compare it with Mather's provisional R/H/F/O observations.
Do not treat any comparison witness as the direct exemplar without codicological or textual proof.
openQuestions.question: Can the lost French exemplar behind the anonymous English translation be identified or placed more securely among the French textual families?

next_steps: Resolve the copy through USTC 501179, ESTC S105285, Bodleian SOLO, and an Early Printed Books enquiry.
If imaging is pursued, request the complete surviving bound object and record the missing A1-2 separately.
openQuestions.question: What is the current shelfmark and exact surviving physical scope of Mather's Bodleian W copy?

next_steps: Use the recorded British Library and Cambridge whole-object request routes, preserving all binding matter, blanks, and adjacent contents.
Do not substitute selected illuminations or Mather's cited passages for complete physical-object coverage.
openQuestions.question: Can complete public or repository-supplied surrogates be secured for Mather's R, H, and F manuscript comparison witnesses?

reviewNotes: The anonymous English work, its physical witnesses, a later English printed version, French source work and comparison witnesses, Mather's edition, embedded plate, digital reproductions, U-M encoding, and repository artifact are distinct entities.
A complete physical-object surrogate is retained whenever publicly obtainable. Dated whole-object negative results and official request routes are recorded for every manuscript witness lacking one.
Caching research files does not grant redistribution or print-on-demand rights; image licenses and underlying-text status remain separate layers.

lineage.summary: CME00068 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic transcription of Frank Jewett Mather Jr.'s 1897 edition of the anonymous Middle English King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone. The encoding covers Mather's edited text and apparatus on printed pp. 1-150, not his introductory pp. i-lxvii or facsimile plate. Mather transcribes Digby 185 as his base, prints two Douce 384 fragments in notes, and supplies a passage corresponding to printed p. 57 after line 13 through p. 60 line 13 from the later 1511 W text because Digby's exemplar had lost a leaf. A newly current complete 424-surface Digby digital object is cached and mapped to ff. 166r-203r / canvases 339-413; its older thirteen-image Digital Bodleian object remains a separately modeled legacy partial selection. No complete public Douce 384 object was found, so the record preserves a dated whole-composite negative result and Bodleian reproduction-order route. The Mather volume, Cambridge public front matter, Bodleian catalog, and complete Ortuin printed French comparison copy are also cached with exact boundaries.

catalogingSubject.label: King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The anonymous Middle English prose translation and adaptation of the French romance Ponthus et Sidoine represented by CME00068. It is distinct from the French source work, the Digby 185 and Douce 384 physical witnesses, Henry Watson's later printed English version, Frank Jewett Mather Jr.'s 1897 scholarly edition, the PMLA volume and its scans, the University of Michigan encoding, and the checked-in repository XML artifact.

contentParts.label: Romance narrative

locators.value: single DIV1; DIV2 N=1-35; PB N=1-150

notes: Mather supplied and bracketed the chapter headings; illuminated or enlarged initials in Digby mark the underlying textual divisions.
contentParts.summary: Thirty-five prose chapters tracing exile, chivalric formation, love and separation, warfare in Brittany and England, the reconquest of Galicia, reunion and marriage, and exemplary kingship.

dateStatements.display: First half of the fifteenth century
notes: The range represents a broad scholarly placement before or around the date of Digby 185, not a dated act of translation.

dateStatements.display: Mid-fifteenth century, in Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Digby 185
notes: This is the earliest surviving complete English witness identified here, not necessarily the date of composition.

dateStatements.display: 1897, in Frank Jewett Mather Jr.'s PMLA edition
notes: The statement concerns first publication of this Digby-based English version; Henry Watson's 1510/1511 printed work is a later revision or retranslation rather than an edition of the same wording.

extent.description: One prose romance represented by thirty-five editorially numbered chapters. The XML has one DIV1 and thirty-five DIV2 chapter containers, 368 P elements, 150 PB elements, 305 NOTE elements, and 986 MILESTONE elements. These encoding and apparatus counts are not intrinsic work-unit counts beyond the thirty-five editorial chapter divisions.

formStatements.description: A continuous prose romance divided by manuscript initials and represented as thirty-five numbered chapters in Mather's edition.

genres.label: Didactic romance
notes: The incipit promises good examples; Ponthus is constructed as an exemplary knight and delivers extended instruction on religion, marriage, lordship, and justice near the conclusion.

languageStatements.label: Middle English
notes: French comparison text and modern English editorial matter belong to Mather's edition and source apparatus, not to the abstract work's primary language.
Mather's introduction and apparatus remain indispensable source evidence but some of his dating, textual genealogy, and unique-witness language require modern qualification.
The work's represented religious warfare is cataloged with contextual language because medieval labels and stereotypes are not neutral descriptions of historical Muslim communities.
Witness-specific geography, complete-object facsimile scope, the legacy selected-image object, reproduction licenses, and print-on-demand permissions belong to the linked lineage manifest.

next_steps: Review the Hunt and Watson attribution rationale and post-1999 Hoccleve scholarship.
Retain anonymous authorship and a separate disputed attribution unless evidence specifically addresses the romance rather than neighboring Hoccleve texts in Digby 185.
openQuestions.question: Is there substantive paleographical, linguistic, or documentary evidence for the 1999 catalog's attribution of this romance to Thomas Hoccleve?

next_steps: Review modern linguistic and textual studies of the English version and distinguish translator language from Digby's copyist language.
Do not promote Yorkshire ownership or dialect evidence to a place of translation without direct support.
openQuestions.question: Can the English translation's date and place be narrowed independently of Digby 185's mid-fifteenth-century copying and Yorkshire scribal layer?

next_steps: Consult modern French and English stemmatic scholarship beyond Mather's provisional R/H/F/O comparisons.
Keep the abstract French source work, Mather's comparison witnesses, and any hypothetical lost exemplar distinct.
openQuestions.question: Can the particular French exemplar behind the anonymous English translation be identified among extant witnesses?

notes: Mather's account of a standard-English translation with a somewhat northernized Yorkshire scribal layer belongs primarily to Digby 185's transmission; it does not securely localize the act of translation to Yorkshire.

scope.description: English prose version whose closeness to the French source varies substantially across the narrative
Digby 185 main text, two Douce 384 fragments in the apparatus, and a lost-exemplar gap supplied from the 1511 Wynkyn de Worde text
Selective readings from W, French manuscripts R, H, and F, and the Ortuin printed French edition

notes: The 1999 addenda to the Digby catalog label item 5 'By Thomas Hoccleve,' but Mather, ARLIMA, and the current item-level component description do not assign the romance to him. Hoccleve's securely identified works elsewhere in the composite codex do not prove authorship of this item.

subjects.label: Representations of Christian-Saracen conflict
notes: The label describes a literary representation shaped by medieval Christian polemic and stereotype; it is not a neutral account of historical Muslim peoples or religions.

summaries.value: After Brodas conquers Galicia and kills King Tiber, the young Ponthus escapes with thirteen companions to Brittany. He wins military renown and Sidone's love, but the envious Guenelete separates them through slander. Ponthus spends seven years in England, returns in disguise to prevent Sidone's forced marriage, reconquers Galicia, and is crowned king. He then rescues Sidone from Guenelete, marries her, arranges Pollides's marriage and English succession, and models charitable, just, and devout lordship until his death.

Mather organizes the romance into thirty-five numbered prose chapters. Broadly, they cover Ponthus's childhood exile and defense of Brittany; his love of Sidone, tournaments, slander, and English exile; and his return, Galician reconquest, defeat of Guenelete, marriages, and exemplary rule. The chapter headings are bracketed editorial supplies based on textual and comparative evidence rather than medieval numbered titles.

CME00068 encodes only Mather's edited Middle English text on printed pp. 1-150, including its footnote apparatus. It excludes Mather's printed pp. i-lxvii introduction, glossary, and plate as primary text. Two Douce 384 fragments are quoted inside notes, and a passage corresponding to printed p. 57 after line 13 through p. 60 line 13 is supplied from the later W text because Digby 185's exemplar had lost a leaf; these editorial layers do not turn the cataloged work into a composite of three independently authored works.

notes: The title is an established editorial and cataloging form; Digby 185 opens without a medieval title rubric at this locus.
titles.supplied_by: General Corpus, following Mather's edition title and current scholarship
titles.value: King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone

notes: This is a current component label, not a transcription of a medieval rubric.

titles.value: Now I wolle you tell a noble storye, wherof a man may lerne mony goode ensamples

notes: The title's 'unique MS' language is best understood as referring to the only complete manuscript then known: Mather separately prints the two Douce 384 fragment leaves in his apparatus.

scope.description: Edition title rather than a medieval title statement

He wins military renown and Sidone's love, but the envious Guenelete separates them through slander. Ponthus spends seven years in England, returns in disguise to prevent Sidone's forced marriage, reconquers Galicia, and is crowned king. He then rescues Sidone from Guenelete, marries her, arranges Pollides's marriage and English succession, and models charitable, just, and devout lordship until his death.
